{'tool_use_id': 'functions.Read:0', 'type': 'tool_result', 'content': '1\t---\n2\ttitle: Claude\n3\ttype: note\n4\tpermalink: ai-memory/claude\n5\t---\n6\t\n7\t# Claude Code Master Context: Gilad\'s AI Stack\n8\t*Last updated: 2026-04-27.*\n9\t*Read this in full before making any changes. Some infrastructure is live and operational. Breaking it has real consequences.*\n10\t\n11\t---\n12\t\n13\t## How to Use This Document\n14\t\n15\tThis document covers three separate, ongoing projects that share infrastructure and personnel (one human, various AI tools) but have distinct goals. **Do not conflate them.** When working on any one project, be aware of the others — not to work on them, but to ensure your actions don\'t create obstacles for them. Where an action can incidentally advance or unblock another project at low cost, note it and flag it to Gilad; don\'t execute it unilaterally.\n16\t\n17\tThe three projects:\n18\t\n19\t| # | Project | One-Line Goal |\n20\t|---|---|---|\n21\t| P1 | **OpenClaw / Aurora** | A self-hosted AI agent (Aurora) deployed on a cloud server, reachable via Telegram/Discord/web, capable of using tools and consuming knowledge from Gilad\'s full stack |\n22\t| P2 | **Shared Memory Pool** | A single MCP-accessible memory layer that every AI tool in the stack can simultaneously read from and write to, providing cross-tool persistent context |\n23\t| P3 | **Long-Term Knowledge Repository** | A living, LLM-powered, ingestion-automated, universally queryable knowledge base for all of Gilad\'s accumulated research, notes, and information |\n24\t\n25\tP2 and P3 share the same vault and MCP infrastructure. P1 will eventually consume from P2/P3. They are not the same project.\n26\t\n27\t---\n28\t\n29\t## Project P1: OpenClaw / Aurora Agentic Infrastructure\n30\t\n31\t### Goal\n32\tAurora is a self-hosted AI assistant running on the Netcup server. It handles Gilad\'s requests via Telegram, Discord, and a web dashboard. The P1 goal is a fully operational, stable, correctly-routed agent that uses the right model at the right tier, can invoke tools (including memory and Google Workspace), and doesn\'t crash or fall back silently.\n33\t\n34\t### Current Status\n35\tOperational. Both active blockers resolved in April 2026-04-25 session:\n36\t- U1 resolved: Trimmed brain files (DREAMS.md archived), disabled dreaming plugin, switched primary to cloud models\n37\t- U2 resolved: Updated to OpenClaw v2026.4.24, MCP tools now surfacing natively. Aurora can read/write basic-memory.\n38\tAurora is reachable via Discord and Telegram, responds in ~3 seconds on primary model.\n39\t\n40\t### Key Components\n41\t\n42\t**OpenClaw binary:** `/home/openclaw/.npm-global/bin/openclaw`\n43\tRun all commands as the `openclaw` user:\n44\t```bash\n45\tsudo -u openclaw bash -c \'export PATH=/home/openclaw/.npm-global/bin:$PATH && openclaw config set ...\'\n46\t```\n47\t\n48\t**Critical config rule:** Never edit `openclaw.json` directly. Always use `openclaw config set`. Exception: `mcporter.json` must be edited directly (see below).\n49\t\n50\t**Model config:**\n51\t- `models.mode=replace` — auto-discovery disabled. Non-negotiable.\n52\t- Primary: `openrouter/google/gemini-2.5-flash-lite` via OpenRouter — fast, reliable, large context\n53\t- Fallback: `openrouter/moonshotai/kimi-k2.6` (200k ctx) — intermittent timeout on large context (>60K tokens), kept as fallback\n54\t  - **Must include `openrouter/` prefix.** Bare `moonshotai/kimi-k2.6` fails because OpenClaw cannot resolve provider `moonshotai` (not configured). See KI-007.\n55\t- ~~Fallback 1: `groq/llama-3.1-8b-instant`~~ — REMOVED: rate-limited (6000 TPM), fails on all large-context requests\n56\t- Ollama Cloud models configured but not in active fallback chain: `minimax-m2.5:cloud`, `qwen3.5:cloud`, `glm-5:cloud`\n57\t- Local Ollama models on Netcup retained for future use: `qwen2.5:1.5b`, `deepseek-r1:1.5b`, `llama3.2:latest`\n58\t\n59\t**Ollama config (do not change):**\n60\t```ini\n61\tOLLAMA_HOST=0.0.0.0\n62\tOLLAMA_NUM_CTX=16384\n63\tOLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS=1\n64\tOLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=5m\n65\tOLLAMA_NUM_THREADS=8\n66\tOLLAMA_FLASH_ATTENTION=1\n67\tOLLAMA_KV_CACHE_TYPE=q8_0\n68\tOLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL=1\n69\t```\n70\tOverride file: `/etc/systemd/system/ollama.service.d/override.conf`\n71\t\n72\t**Ollama models installed (do not add to this list without explicit approval):**\n73\t| Model | Size | Role |\n74\t|---|---|---|\n75\t| `qwen2.5:1.5b` | ~1GB | Primary |\n76\t| `deepseek-r1:1.5b` | ~1.1GB | Reasoning fallback |\n77\t| `llama3.2:latest` | 2.0GB | Secondary |\n78\t\n79\t**Aurora brain files:** `/home/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace/` (`SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `AGENTS.md`)\n80\t**Per-agent brain files:** `/home/openclaw/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/` — these load at runtime and consume context budget. Total word count must stay under ~1500 words or context budget overflows and Aurora times out.\n81\t**Session store:** `/home/openclaw/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json` — delete this file directly to reset session state. `openclaw sessions clear` does not exist.\n82\t\n83\t**Composio (Google Workspace integration):**\n84\t- Plugin: `@composio/openclaw-plugin`, consumer key `ck_zQAH84mLsnNDinbhs3qk`\n85\t- Managed at `dashboard.composio.dev` — browser-based auth only. Never attempt CLI OAuth on the server (every invocation generates a new PKCE challenge that immediately invalidates the previous token).\n86\t\n87\t**mcporter (MCP tool invocation layer):**\n88\tConfig at `~/.mcporter/mcporter.json` — this takes precedence over `/home/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace/config/mcporter.json`. Always edit the home-directory file. Edit directly; `openclaw config set` does not manage this file.\n89\tSyntax uses dot notation for server/tool and colon notation for arguments:\n90\t```\n91\tbasic-memory.search_notes query:"search term"\n92\tbasic-memory.write_note title:"Title" content:"content"\n93\t```\n94\t\n95\t### P1 Roadmap\n96\t- [x] OpenClaw deployed on Netcup\n97\t- [x] Telegram channel working\n98\t- [x] Ollama installed and tuned; OOM issue resolved\n99\t- [x] **Fix U1:** Aurora falling back to Gemini — resolved via cloud model switch + brain file trim\n100\t- [x] **Fix U2:** OpenClaw version bug blocking MCP tool surfacing — resolved via v2026.4.24 update\n101\t- [x] OpenClaw ↔ basic-memory integration (MCP read/write verified)\n102\t- [x] Discord migration (active, groupPolicy=allowlist)\n103\t- [x] ~~Open WebUI formally adopted as primary chat UI~~ — DECISION: Discord/Telegram remain primary interfaces. Open WebUI runs on port 3000→8080 as optional SSH-tunnel access only.\n104\t\n105\t### P1-Specific Obstacles Solved\n106\t**OOM Crashes (A1):** `models.mode=replace` + hardcoded context windows + large model removal. See model config above.\n107\t**Config Mutation Bug (A2):** A known OpenClaw bug writes the active fallback model back into config. Clear by deleting `sessions.json` directly.\n108\t**Composio OAuth on Headless Server (A3):** Use `dashboard.composio.dev` browser flow only.\n109\t**Docker→Host Connectivity (A4):** Use `--add-host=host.docker.internal:host-gateway` and `http://host.docker.internal:11434` in container config.\n110\t**CLI User Context (A5):** All OpenClaw commands must run as the `openclaw` user with explicit PATH export.\n111\t\n112\t---\n113\t\n114\t## Project P2: Shared Memory Pool\n115\t\n116\t### Goal\n117\tA single, always-on MCP endpoint that every AI tool in Gilad\'s stack — Claude Desktop, Claude.ai web, Claude mobile, Claude Code, OpenClaw, Perplexity, ChatGPT, Gemini, and others — can connect to simultaneously. Any tool can read from it and write to it. Cross-tool persistent context becomes possible: a decision made in Claude.ai is visible to OpenClaw; a note written by Claude Code is queryable by Perplexity.\n118\t\n119\t### Current Status\n120\tCore infrastructure live. Claude Desktop, Claude.ai web, Claude mobile, Claude Code, Aurora (OpenClaw), Gemini CLI, and Perplexity are connected and working. Manus connected and tested. Genspark blocked by UI bug. ChatGPT limited to read-only on Plus/Pro.\n121\t\n122\t### The MCP Endpoint\n123\t```\n124\thttps://memory.giladn.com/mcp/ebc69cbd338019004dfd1738a033ced1e22e21ff62a41b41ef0b8caf5d9fb3a5\n125\t```\n126\tThis URL is the API key. Treat it as a credential. Authentication is path-based — no header-based auth. Any tool that supports remote MCP connects by pointing at this URL.\n127\t\n128\t### Connection Status\n129\t| Tool | Connection Method | Status |\n130\t|---|---|---|\n131\t| Claude Desktop (Mac) | Local stdio MCP | ✅ Working |\n132\t| Claude.ai web | Remote MCP via URL | ✅ Working |\n133\t| Claude mobile app | Remote MCP via URL | ✅ Working |\n134\t| Claude Code | Local vault + remote MCP | Connected |\n135\t| OpenClaw | Native MCP + mcporter fallback | Connected |\n136\t| Perplexity | mcp-remote stdio bridge | Connected |\n137\t| Manus | Streamable HTTP direct | Connected |\n138\t| Genspark | Streamable HTTP direct | 🚫 Blocked by UI bug |\n139\t| ChatGPT | Streamable HTTP (read-only on Plus/Pro) | Limited |\n140\t| Gemini | Google Docs bridge | ❌ Architecture designed; not built |\n141\t\n142\t### The basic-memory MCP Server\n143\tbasic-memory (v0.20.3) is the MCP server layer. It indexes the AI-Memory vault\'s Markdown files and exposes read/write tools to connected AI clients.\n144\t\n145\t**Installation:** Under the `openclaw` user on Netcup. Also installed locally on Mac for Claude Desktop.\n146\t**Config (server):** `~/.basic-memory/config.json` — `"default_project": "ai-memory"` (kebab-case). Never edit the SQLite DB (`memory.db`) directly — JSON config takes precedence and resets DB state on startup.\n147\t**Config (Mac):** `~/.basic-memory/config.json` — same setting.\n148\t**Systemd service:** Runs on port 8765. nginx proxies it to `memory.giladn.com`.\n149\t\n150\t```bash\n151\t# Key commands:\n152\tbasic-memory tool search-notes "query term"   # positional arg, NOT --query flag\n153\tbasic-memory reindex\n154\tbasic-memory mcp --transport streamable-http --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8765 --project AI-Memory\n155\t\n156\t# Health check — this is CORRECT behavior, not an error:\n157\t# {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":"server-error","error":{"code":-32600,"message":"Not Acceptable: Client must accept text/event-stream"}}\n158\t```\n159\t\n160\t### P2 Roadmap\n161\t- [x] basic-memory installed on Mac and Netcup\n162\t- [x] nginx + Cloudflare SSL; remote MCP live at memory.giladn.com\n163\t- [x] Claude Desktop, Claude.ai web/mobile connected\n164\t- [x] Connect Claude Code (local + remote)\n165\t- [x] Connect Perplexity, Manus (MCP config complete)\n166\t- [ ] Connect Genspark (blocked by UI bug)\n167\t- [ ] Connect ChatGPT (read-only on Plus/Pro — low priority)\n168\t- [x] Fix OpenClaw ↔ basic-memory (resolved in v2026.4.24)\n169\t- [ ] Build Gemini Google Docs bridge (architecture exists; no code written)\n170\t- [x] Git push hook on Mac for instant sync (LaunchAgent com.giladnass.ai-memory-sync operational)\n171\t\n172\t### P2-Specific Obstacles Solved\n173\t**basic-memory CLI syntax (B1):** Positional arg only; kebab-case project name in config.\n174\t**SQLite vs JSON config (B2):** Never edit the DB. JSON config is authoritative.\n175\t**mcporter syntax (B3):** Dot + colon notation. Home-directory `mcporter.json` takes precedence.\n176\t**OpenClaw defaulting to Composio (B4):** Unsolved — pending P1/U2 version fix.\n177\t**File creation with special characters (B6):** Use Python scripts, not heredocs (backticks break heredocs).\n178\t**NotebookLM not in Drive API (B7):** Use `notebooklm-mcp` or browser automation. Drive API returns empty results for NotebookLM notebooks.\n179\t\n180\t### Gemini Bridge Architecture (Designed, Not Built)\n181\tTwo Google Docs: an **Inbox** doc and a **Context** doc. A bridge script reads the vault → populates the Context doc. Gemini writes outputs to the Inbox doc → bridge script ingests those back into the vault. Zero code written; flagged for future implementation.\n182\t\n183\t---\n184\t\n185\t## Project P3: Long-Term Knowledge Repository\n186\t\n187\t### Goal\n188\tA living, self-compounding, LLM-powered knowledge base. Everything Gilad reads, watches, or researches gets ingested, converted to structured Markdown, tagged, summarized, cross-linked, and made queryable — by any AI tool, at any time, from any device. Inspired by Andrey Karpathy\'s LLM Wiki concept. The end state is a fully automated ingestion pipeline: drop a PDF, paste a URL, record a voice note → it lands in the vault, processed and linked, without manual work.\n189\t\n190\t### Current Status\n191\tThe vault and its access layer (P2) are live. The ingestion automation layer is operational since April 21-24. Conversion pipeline (ingest.py, process.py, watch.sh, transcribe.py, lint.py, session_end.py, linkding_export.py) is processing files. Vault is no longer manually populated — sources drop into `01-source/` and are auto-converted, processed, and committed.\n192\t\n193\t### The Vault\n194\t\n195\t**The only vault is AI-Memory. There are no other vaults.**\n196\t\n197\t| Location | Path |\n198\t|---|---|\n199\t| Mac (primary, iCloud-backed) | `/Users/giladnass/Library/Mobile Documents/iCloud~md~obsidian/Documents/AI-Memory` |\n200\t| Netcup (server clone, Git-synced) | `/home/openclaw/AI-Memory` |\n201\t| GitHub (sync hub) | `https://github.com/giladnass/Repo1` |\n202\t\n203\t**Sync mechanism:**\n204\t- Mac: LaunchAgent `com.giladnass.ai-memory-sync` polls every 10s, commits, pulls (rebase), and pushes\n205\t- Netcup: 5-minute cron job (`openclaw` user) pulls from GitHub with stash+pull+pop hardening\n206\t- Both directions covered; Mac push is instant, Netcup pull is near-real-time\n207\t\n208\t**Vault structure (do not alter):**\n209\t```\n210\tAI-Memory/\n211\t├── 000-Meta/          # Schema definitions, session logs, handoff notes, index\n212\t├── Wiki/              # Permanent knowledge pages (the "library")\n213\t├── Sources/           # Raw/processed source material (immutable originals)\n214\t├── Working-Context/   # Active project roadmaps, session handoffs\n215\t├── Templates/         # Frontmatter templates (wiki-page, weekly-review, source-note)\n216\t└── Memory/            # Persistent AI-facing context (preferences, tool configs)\n217\t```\n218\tSchema is defined in `000-Meta/CLAUDE.md`. All files: YAML frontmatter, `[[wikilinks]]`, tags.\n219\t\n220\t### Ingestion Pipeline (Operational)\n221\t\n222\t**Core architectural decision:** All source material is pre-converted to Markdown before ingestion. Raw binary formats (PDF, DOCX, MP4, etc.) are never fed directly to basic-memory. MD is inspectable, correctable, portable, and Git-versionable.\n223\t\n224\t**Processing split:**\n225\t- Lightweight conversions (PDF, Office docs, EPUB) → run on Mac\n226\t- Heavy compute (audio transcription, video frame/slide extraction) → run on Netcup overnight\n227\t\n228\t**Tools installed and operational:**\n229\t| Tool | Purpose | Run On | Status |\n230\t|---|---|---|---|\n231\t| `pymupdf4llm` | PDF → Markdown | Mac | Operational (D-009) |\n232\t| `pandoc` | DOCX / PPTX / EPUB → Markdown | Mac | Operational |\n233\t| `faster-whisper` | Audio/video transcription | Netcup | Operational |\n234\t| `linkding` | Self-hosted bookmark manager | Netcup | Operational |\n235\t| Claude API (batch) | Content triage, tagging, summarization | Cloud | Operational |\n236\t\n237\t**Suspended / to evaluate:**\n238\t| Tool | Status | Reason |\n239\t|---|---|---|\n240\t| `marker` | Suspended (KI-005) | Apple Silicon MPS bug |\n241\t| `docling` (IBM) | To evaluate | Potential replacement for marker |\n242\t| `ArchiveBox` / Firecrawl | To evaluate | Web page archival |\n243\t| `DePlot` / `ChartOCR` | To evaluate | Chart extraction |\n244\t\n245\t### P3 Roadmap\n246\t- [x] Vault created with full folder structure and schema\n247\t- [x] GitHub repo created; Git sync between Mac and Netcup operational\n248\t- [x] Install and test ingestion conversion tools (pymupdf4llm, pandoc, faster-whisper)\n249\t- [x] File watcher on Mac (`fswatch` via `watch.sh` LaunchAgent)\n250\t- [x] Routing logic: full ingest vs. summary-only vs. index-only (process.py triage step)\n251\t- [ ] Google Drive webhook → auto-ingest new files\n252\t- [x] Install `faster-whisper` on Netcup; scheduled audio/video queue\n253\t- [x] Claude API batch processing for content triage and tagging (process.py)\n254\t- [x] Claude conversation capture: end-of-session Claude Code write-to-vault routine (session_end.py)\n255\t- [x] Seed vault from existing NotebookLM notebooks and research outputs (PDF batch processed)\n256\t- [x] Define "contribution contract": entry format that works across all connected tools (D-006 active)\n257\t- [x] Git push hook on Mac for instant sync on file save (LaunchAgent operational)\n258\t\n259\t### P3-Specific Unresolved Problem\n260\t**Claude conversation capture (U6):** Claude.ai has no official export API, so planning sessions are currently lost unless manually copied. The recommended approach: Claude Code sessions are the primary vehicle for knowledge-building work (Claude Code has full filesystem access and can write directly to the vault). A systematic end-of-session save routine needs to be designed.\n261\t\n262\t---\n263\t\n264\t## Shared Infrastructure\n265\t\n266\tBoth P2 and P3 depend on the following. Changes here affect both projects — plan accordingly.\n267\t\n268\t### Netcup Server\n269\t- **OS**: Ubuntu 24.04 | **RAM**: 32GB | **CPU**: 12 vCPUs (AMD EPYC) | **No GPU**\n270\t- **Public IP**: `152.53.244.42` | **Tailscale IP**: `100.127.32.127`\n271\t- **SSH**: `Host netcup` alias in `~/.ssh/config` on Mac\n272\t\n273\t**All running services:**\n274\t| Service | User | Port | Notes |\n275\t|---|---|---|---|\n276\t| OpenClaw gateway (P1) | `openclaw` | internal | `systemctl --user restart openclaw-gateway` as `openclaw` user |\n277\t| basic-memory MCP (P2/P3) | `openclaw` | 8765 | systemd auto-start |\n278\t| nginx (P2/P3) | root | 80/443 | Reverse proxy for memory.giladn.com |\n279\t| Ollama (P1) | root | 11434 | `OLLAMA_HOST=0.0.0.0` |\n280\t| AnythingLLM (P1) | root | 3001 (localhost) | SSH tunnel only |\n281\t| Open WebUI (P1) | root | 3000→8080 | `ssh -L 3003:127.0.0.1:3000` |\n282\t| IPTVX | iptvx | 9191 (public) | Unrelated; do not touch |\n283\t\n284\t### Mac\n285\t- **Machine**: MacBook Pro M4, 48GB unified memory\n286\t- **Inference**: 50–80 tokens/sec (mid-size models) — use for speed-sensitive tasks\n287\t- **Netcup inference**: 30–50 tokens/sec (sub-2B models only) — use for always-on/background tasks\n288\t\n289\t### NotebookLM\n290\tUsed as Gilad\'s primary research synthesis layer before bringing findings to Claude Code for execution. Key notebooks: "Deploying Openclaw on Netcup", "Local LLMs". Access via `notebooklm-mcp`. Use `notebook_query` with `timeout: 90`. Cannot be accessed via Google Drive API — browser/MCP only.\n291\t\n292\t### CoWork\n293\tDesktop Claude client on Mac. `/schedule` for scheduled tasks. Only runs when Mac is awake. **Files are the only reliable inter-tool bridge** — do not design workflows that depend on real-time communication between claude.ai, Claude Code, CoWork, and OpenClaw.\n294\t\n295\t---\n296\t\n297\t## Active Unresolved Problems\n298\t\n299\t### U3: Aurora Memory Writes Fixed [P1]\n300\tDaily memory files (`/home/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace/memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`) are the intended mechanism for cross-session context. Root cause: `memory/` directory did not exist. Fixed by creating directory, writing seed `2026-04-26.md`, and adding explicit daily persistence rules to `AGENTS.md`.\n301\t\n302\t### U4: Gemini Google Docs Bridge [P2]\n303\tScript `gemini_bridge.py` written and activated. OAuth configured; Context + Inbox docs created. Push/pull verified. Needs Drive API enabled in Google Cloud project.\n304\t\n305\t### U5: Ingestion Pipeline — Refinements [P3]\n306\tCore pipeline operational. Remaining gaps:\n307\t- Google Drive webhook auto-ingest not built\n308\t- `02-converted/` cleanup after process.py runs (partially done — only 2 dirs remain as of 2026-04-25)\n309\t- Visual element preservation strategy not designed\n310\t\n311\t### U6: Claude Conversation Capture [P3]\n312\tPartially solved via `session_end.py`. Claude.ai still has no export API. Claude Code sessions can write directly to vault. A systematic end-of-session save routine exists but is not automated.\n313\t\n314\t---\n315\t\n316\t## Non-Negotiable Rules\n317\t\n318\tThese apply across all three projects. Violations cause immediate or cascading breakage.\n319\t\n320\t1. **Do not change the AI-Memory vault folder structure.** Schema in `000-Meta/CLAUDE.md` governs it; basic-memory is indexed against it.\n321\t2. **Do not edit `~/.basic-memory/memory.db` directly.** JSON config is authoritative.\n322\t3. **Do not edit `openclaw.json` directly.** Use `openclaw config set` as the `openclaw` user.\n323\t4. **`models.mode=replace` must stay set.** Auto-discovery is a catastrophic failure mode on this hardware.\n324\t5. **All OpenClaw commands must run as the `openclaw` user** with explicit PATH: `sudo -u openclaw bash -c \'export PATH=/home/openclaw/.npm-global/bin:$PATH && ...\'`\n325\t6. **`mcporter.json` must be edited directly** at `~/.mcporter/mcporter.json` — not via `openclaw config set`, not the workspace config path.\n326\t7. **The MCP URL is the API key.** Do not log it or hardcode it in committed files.\n327\t8. **Use Python scripts, not heredocs,** for any file creation containing Markdown code blocks.\n328\t9. **The MCP health-check `"Not Acceptable"` curl response is correct.** Do not try to fix it.\n329\t10. **Vault syncs via Git.** Files written to AI-Memory on Mac must be committed and pushed to reach Netcup. Files written on Netcup are pulled by cron every 5 minutes.\n330\t11. **Do not add Ollama models without explicit approval.** The model set is intentionally minimal.\n331\t12. **`openclaw sessions clear` does not exist.** Delete `sessions.json` directly.\n332\t13. **Context window math is critical.** At 2048 tokens: system prompt + brain files + tool definitions + conversation history + user message must all fit. Any unchecked growth breaks the budget.\n333\t14. **Files are the only reliable inter-tool bridge.** No real-time communication assumptions.\n334\t15. **AI-Memory is the only vault.** Do not create, reference, or configure any additional vault unless explicitly instructed and documented here first.\n335\t\n336\t---\n337\t\n338\t*Last updated: 2026-04-27.*\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n'}

## assistant (2026-05-01T09:05:08.249Z)

OpenClaw Theme Source Code\n**File:** [`openclaw/openclaw/ui/src/ui/theme.ts`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/ui/src/ui/theme.ts)\n- Defines OpenClaw\'s own theme system with three built-in themes: `claw`, `knot`, and `dash`\n- Contains theme resolution logic, dark/light mode handling, and legacy theme mappings\n\n```typescript\nexport type ThemeName = "claw" | "knot" | "dash";\nexport type ThemeMode = "system" | "light" | "dark";\n\n// Legacy map for backwards compatibility\nconst LEGACY_MAP: Record<string, ThemeSelection> = {\n  defaultTheme: { theme: "claw", mode: "dark" },\n  openknot: { theme: "knot", mode: "dark" },\n  fieldmanual: { theme: "dash", mode: "dark" },\n  // ... etc\n};\n```\n\n## 3. OpenClaw shadcn/ui Theme Skill\n**Repository:** [`openclaw/skills`](https://github.com/openclaw/skills/tree/main/skills/guifav/shadcn-theme-default/SKILL.md)\n- Contains detailed documentation (`SKILL.md`) for enforcing the default shadcn/ui Neutral theme\n- Uses OKLCH CSS variables and Tailwind v4 `@theme inline` directives (same color format as tweakcn)\n- Includes complete `globals.css` template with light/dark mode variables\n\n---\n\n**Note:** The search did not reveal explicit source code showing a direct import bridge between OpenClaw and tweakcn. The two projects appear to use similar modern shadcn/ui theme architectures (OKLCH + Tailwind v4 CSS variables), but operate independently. The search did **not** return any results explicitly mentioning "tweakcn" within the `github.com/openclaw` domain. \n\nHowever, it did return several recent results from the **`openclaw/openclaw`** repository related to `import fetch` patterns, compatibility shims, and fetch infrastructure:\n\n### 1. PR #47914 — gaxios-fetch-compat shim\n**Title:** `fix(infra): wire gaxios-fetch-compat shim to prevent node-fetch crash on Node.js 25`  \n**URL:** https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/47914  \n- Adds a side-effect import `"./infra/gaxios-fetch-compat.js"` to `src/index.ts` and `src/entry.ts`.\n- The shim sets `globalThis.window = { fetch: globalThis.fetch }` so that `gaxios@7` uses Node\'s native fetch instead of lazily importing `node-fetch@3.x`, which crashes on Node.js 25.\n\n### 2. File: `extensions/telegram/src/fetch.ts`\n**URL:** https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/9098e948/extensions/telegram/src/fetch.ts  \n- Implements a custom Telegram transport using `undici`\'s `fetch` (`import { fetch as undiciFetch } from "undici"`).\n- Wraps and resolves fetch implementations with dispatcher fallback logic for proxies and VPN environments.\n\n### 3. File: `src/media/fetch.ts`\n**URL:** https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/9098e948/src/media/fetch.ts  \n- Contains media fetching utilities.\n- Imports `fetchWithSsrFGuard` and related network guard modules (`import { fetchWithSsrFGuard, ... } from "../infra/net/fetch-guard.js"`).\n\n### 4. Issue #33013 — Telegram undici bypass\n**URL:** https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/33013  \n- Discusses how the Telegram module switched from Node.js native `fetch` to `undici`\'s fetch, causing VPN/TUN bypass issues on macOS.\n- Includes workarounds and mentions reverting to native fetch.\n\n### 5. Issue #41380 — Google Chat JWT / node-fetch import failure\n**URL:** https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/41380  \n- Documents a failure where `gaxios` dynamically tries `import(\'node-fetch\')` under pnpm strict isolation.\n- Provides a workaround that sets `globalThis.window` to force use of native fetch.\n\n---\n\n**Summary:** There are no visible matches for the term **"tweakcn"** in the `github.com/openclaw` domain based on this search. ## assistant (2026-05-01T09:11:37.055Z)
> Thinking: Both `/api/theme/` and `/api/export/` just return the same HTML page (Next.js fallback routing). So there's definitely no public API endpoint for themes.

Now, the question is: what app is the user actually using? The screenshot shows what appears to be an OpenClaw-related UI. But the OpenClaw repo ...

## assistant (2026-05-01T09:11:40.218Z)
Two problems.

**1. CORS.** tweakcn.com sends no `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` header. Any browser fetch from different origin (localhost, your app domain) gets blocked before response body is read.

**2. No JSON API.** tweakcn pages return HTML (Next.js). No `/api/themes/[id]` or `/api/export/[id]` endpoint exists — both return same HTML fallback.

Your app fetches tweakcn URL expecting JSON, gets either CORS error or HTML parse fail. "Failed to fetch" is the result.

**Workaround:** Open theme on tweakcn.com, copy generated CSS, paste directly into your theme config. No import bridge exists currently.

What app is this UI? OpenClaw repo has no tweakcn references — might be fork/custom build. If you want, I can look at local source to fix importer.
